hypothesis: Incorporating a Dirichlet-process-based noise schedule into the forward diffusion process—replacing the standard Gaussian noise with Dirichlet-distributed perturbations that inherently respect the simplex geometry of compositional data—will simultaneously satisfy non-negativity and sum-to-one constraints without requiring explicit regularization terms, thereby producing generated composite compositions with higher validity and greater coverage of the feasible design space compared to PCDiff's penalty-based approach.
